# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical line drawing of a **wood-framed wall section** shown in perspective. It illustrates **top plates**, **studs**, and rules for **boring holes** and **cutting notches** in wall studs. Several callouts with arrows point to specific parts of the framing members.

## Main Components Shown

- A framed wall with:
  - **Top plates** at the top
  - Multiple vertical **studs**
  - A bottom horizontal plate
- The wall is drawn in a simple isometric/perspective style.
- Some studs contain:
  - **Bored holes**
  - **Notches**
- Arrows and text explain allowable limits for these modifications.

## Visible Text in the Image

### Labels
- **TOP PLATES**
- **STUD**

### Notes and Requirements
- **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**
- **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**
- **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**
- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**
- **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLE AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED**

## What the Diagram Shows

### Top of Wall
At the top of the wall, a callout labeled **TOP PLATES** points to the double horizontal members running along the top edge of the framed wall.

### Studs
A callout labeled **STUD** points to one of the vertical framing members.

### Bored Hole Rule
On the left side of the wall, a circular hole through a stud is identified with the note:

- **BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**

Near this hole, another note states:

- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

This indicates the hole must maintain at least **5/8 inch minimum distance to the edge** of the stud.

### Notch Rule
Lower on the left side, a notch cut into a stud is identified with the note:

- **NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH**

Another note nearby states:

- **BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD**

This indicates that a bored hole and a cut/notch must not occur at the same cross-sectional location in the stud.

### Larger Hole Condition
On the right side of the wall, another bored hole is shown in a stud with the note:

- **5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE**

A longer note points to a stud near the bottom right:

- **IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLE AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED**

This describes the condition under which larger holes are allowed, provided the stud is doubled and the number of successive doubled-and-bored studs is limited.

## Table of Visible Text

| Category | Text |
|---|---|
| Label | TOP PLATES |
| Label | STUD |
| Hole rule | BORED HOLE MAX. DIAMETER 40 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH |
| Edge clearance | 5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE |
| Notch rule | NOTCH MUST NOT EXCEED 25 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH |
| Hole/notch restriction | BORED HOLES SHALL NOT BE LOCATED IN THE SAME CROSS SECTION OF CUT OR NOTCH IN STUD |
| Edge clearance | 5/8 IN. MIN. TO EDGE |
| Larger hole condition | IF HOLE IS BETWEEN 40 PERCENT AND 60 PERCENT OF STUD DEPTH, THEN STUD MUST BE DOUBLE AND NO MORE THAN TWO SUCCESSIVE STUDS ARE DOUBLED AND SO BORED |

## Overall Interpretation

This is a framing guideline diagram showing allowable **hole drilling** and **notching** in wood wall studs, including:
- maximum hole size,
- minimum edge distance,
- maximum notch size,
- prohibition against aligning holes with notches in the same cross section,
- and reinforcement requirements when larger holes are used.

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical construction diagram showing a section of a framed wall with **top plates**, a **pipe**, and a reinforced **notch** in the plate beneath an **exterior or bearing wall**.

## Visible Labels and Text

All visible text in the image:

- **EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL**
- **NOTCH GREATER THAN 50 PERCENT OF THE PLATE WIDTH**
- **TOP PLATES**
- **PIPE**
- **16 GAGE (0.054 IN.) AND 1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE FASTENED ACROSS AND TO THE PLATE AT EACH SIDE OF THE NOTCH WITH 8-10d NAILS EACH SIDE**

## Detailed Visual Description

- A long horizontal/diagonal rectangular framing member is shown near the top of the image, labeled **EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL**.
- Beneath it is a double-layered framing member labeled **TOP PLATES**.
- A **notch** is cut into the top plates where a vertical **pipe** passes through.
- The notch is identified by the note:
  - **NOTCH GREATER THAN 50 PERCENT OF THE PLATE WIDTH**
- The **pipe** is drawn as a vertical cylindrical element passing upward through the notched area.
- On both sides of the notch, narrow rectangular metal straps are shown attached across the cut area.
- A callout points to these straps with the note:
  - **16 GAGE (0.054 IN.) AND 1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE FASTENED ACROSS AND TO THE PLATE AT EACH SIDE OF THE NOTCH WITH 8-10d NAILS EACH SIDE**
- Several vertical wall studs are shown below the top plates, indicating standard wall framing.
- Arrows from the labels point directly to the relevant components:
  - **EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL** points to the upper framing member.
  - **TOP PLATES** points to the double plate assembly.
  - **PIPE** points to the vertical pipe.
  - The notch note points to the cut-out section in the plate.
  - The metal tie note points to the reinforcing straps spanning the notch.

## Interpreted Purpose of the Diagram

The diagram appears to illustrate a framing requirement for when a pipe creates a **notch greater than 50 percent of the plate width** in the **top plates** of an **exterior or bearing wall**. In that condition, reinforcement is required using:

- **16 GAGE (0.054 IN.)**
- **1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE**
- Fastened **across and to the plate at each side of the notch**
- With **8-10d NAILS EACH SIDE**

## Table of Visible Text and Referenced Elements

|---|---|
| EXTERIOR OR BEARING WALL | The upper wall/framing member |

| TOP PLATES | The double horizontal framing plates |
| PIPE | The vertical pipe passing through the wall framing |
| 16 GAGE (0.054 IN.) AND 1.5 IN. WIDE METAL TIE FASTENED ACROSS AND TO THE PLATE AT EACH SIDE OF THE NOTCH WITH 8-10d NAILS EACH SIDE | The metal reinforcement straps attached across the notch |

## Key Values Preserved from the Image

- **Greater than 50 percent of the plate width**
- **16 GAGE**
- **(0.054 IN.)**
- **1.5 IN. wide**
- **8-10d nails each side**

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical construction diagram containing **three labeled figures** related to **header and rim board header construction** in wall framing.

---

## Top Left Diagram

This diagram shows a framed wall/header detail with a **single member header in an exterior bearing wall**. The drawing is in perspective and includes labeled framing members.

### Visible labels
- **TOP PLATE**
- **CRIPPLE**
- **PLATE TO HEADER CONNECTION IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION R602.7.1**
- **JACK STUD**

### Figure caption
- **FIGURE R602.7.1(1)**
- **SINGLE MEMBER HEADER IN EXTERIOR BEARING WALL**

### Description
The framing assembly includes:
- A horizontal **top plate** at the top.
- A horizontal header member below the top plate.
- A short vertical member labeled **cripple** between the top plate and header.
- A vertical support member labeled **jack stud** beneath the header.
- A note pointing to the connection area at the end of the header reading:
  - **PLATE TO HEADER CONNECTION IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION R602.7.1**

---

## Top Right Diagram

This diagram shows an **alternative single member header without cripple**. It is also drawn in perspective and is similar to the left diagram, but without the cripple member.

### Visible labels
- **TOP PLATE**
- **PLATE TO HEADER CONNECTION IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION R602.7.1**
- **JACK STUD**

### Figure caption
- **FIGURE R602.7.1(2)**
- **ALTERNATIVE SINGLE MEMBER HEADER WITHOUT CRIPPLE**

### Description
The framing assembly includes:
- A horizontal **top plate** at the top.
- A horizontal header below.
- A vertical **jack stud** supporting the header.
- A note pointing to the end connection between plate and header:
  - **PLATE TO HEADER CONNECTION IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION R602.7.1**
- Unlike the left figure, there is **no cripple member** shown.

---

## Bottom Large Diagram

This is a larger perspective framing diagram showing **rim board header construction** over an opening in a wall, with floor framing above.

### Visible labels
- **RIM BOARD HEADER SPLICES ARE NOT PERMITTED OVER THE HEADER SPAN**
- **SINGLE-PLY OR TWO-PLY RIM BOARD HEADER AS REQUIRED**
- **JOIST HANGER REQUIRED WHERE BEARING DISTANCE IS < 1½ INCHES**
- **FLOOR JOIST**
- **RIM BOARD HEADER END OR SPLICE 6 INCHES PAST OUTER FULL-HEIGHT STUD**
- **ONE OR MORE FULL-HEIGHT STUDS IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION R602.7.2**
- **HEADER SPAN**
- **CRIPPLES**

### Figure caption
- **FIGURE R602.7.2**
- **RIM BOARD HEADER CONSTRUCTION**

### Additional note
- **For SI: 25.4 mm = 1 inch.**

This diagram depicts:
- A framed wall with a large opening.
- A **rim board header** at the top of the wall opening.
- Several **floor joists** resting perpendicular to the rim board/header assembly.
- **Joist hangers** shown at joist connections where required.
- Vertical wall framing below, including:
  - **one or more full-height studs**
  - **cripples**
- A labeled **header span** across the opening.
- A note indicating that:
  - **RIM BOARD HEADER SPLICES ARE NOT PERMITTED OVER THE HEADER SPAN**
- Another note specifying:
  - **RIM BOARD HEADER END OR SPLICE 6 INCHES PAST OUTER FULL-HEIGHT STUD**

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical diagram containing **two plan-view drawings** side by side. Both drawings show **typical braced wall plans** with labeled braced wall lines, spacing dimensions, maximum offsets, and notes.

---

## Left Diagram: **TYPICAL BRACED WALL PLAN**

This diagram shows an irregular building outline with multiple wall segments and labeled braced wall lines.

### Title
**TYPICAL BRACED WALL PLAN**

### Top labels
Across the top of the diagram are three labeled vertical reference lines:

- **BWL A**
- **BWL B**
- **BWL C**

Between these are spacing labels:

- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL A-B**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL B-C**

### Left side labels
Along the left side are horizontal braced wall line labels:

- **BWL 1**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 1-3**
- **BWL 3**

### Right side labels
Along the right side are horizontal braced wall line labels and spacing labels:

- **BWL 1**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 1-2**
- **BWL 2**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 2-4**
- **BWL 4**

### Visible dimension/limit text
Several arrows indicate maximum offsets or distances, each labeled:

- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**

These appear at:
- a vertical offset near the upper-left interior wall segment,
- a horizontal offset near the left-middle projection,
- a vertical offset near the upper stepped projection,
- a horizontal offset near the center interior wall,
- a horizontal offset near the lower-center interior wall,
- a vertical offset near the lower-left stepped projection.

### Drawing features
- The outer wall perimeter is irregular, with:
  - a stepped projection near the top-left,
  - an angled inset/projection on the left side,
  - a small rectangular projection near the bottom-left,
  - a lower extension downward near the center-right.
- Heavy black wall lines define the perimeter and some interior braced wall segments.
- Circular filled markers appear at intersections of braced wall lines and perimeter/reference lines.
- Dashed vertical reference lines align with **BWL A**, **BWL B**, and **BWL C**.
- Dashed horizontal reference lines align with **BWL 1**, **BWL 2**, **BWL 3**, and **BWL 4**.
- Interior braced wall segments include:
  - a vertical segment descending from near the top center,
  - a horizontal segment extending from the center toward the right wall,
  - a short vertical segment near the lower center.

---

## Right Diagram: **TYPICAL UPPER FLOOR BRACED WALL PLAN**

This diagram shows a more rectangular upper-floor plan with notes about braced wall lines and alignment.

### Title
**TYPICAL UPPER FLOOR BRACED WALL PLAN**

### Top labels
Across the top are three labeled vertical reference lines:

- **BWL A**
- **BWL B**
- **BWL C**

Between these are spacing labels:

- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL A-B**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL B-C**

### Left side labels
Along the left side are vertical notes and labels:

- **BWL 1**
- **SPACING BETWEEN BWL 1 AND END OF BUILDING**
- **END OF BUILDING**

### Visible dimension/limit text
The diagram includes several maximum offset labels:

- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**
- **4' MAX**

### Main note inside the plan
A large note appears inside the central area:

**NO BRACED WALL LINE;  
ROOF AND FLOOR BEAR  
AT SAME ELEVATION**

### Bottom note
A note appears below the plan:

**NOTE: IN THE ABSENCE OF A  
BRACED WALL LINE, BWL A, B,  
C SHALL END AT EXTERIOR WALL**

### Drawing features
- The upper floor perimeter is mostly rectangular.
- The top wall runs horizontally from left to right.
- The bottom wall also runs horizontally from left to right.
- Vertical exterior walls appear at left and right ends.
- Dashed vertical reference lines align with **BWL A**, **BWL B**, and **BWL C**.
- A dashed rectangular outline appears below the lower-right portion of the plan.
- A dashed rectangular outline also appears near the upper-left interior area.
- Interior braced wall segments include:
  - a short vertical segment descending from near **BWL B** at the top,

# Image Description

The image is a black-and-white technical diagram showing a building wall outline with annotations related to **braced wall lines** and **projected length of bracing**.

## Overall Layout

- The main shape is an irregular building perimeter drawn with double-line wall outlines.
- The bottom and right sides are straight.
- The left side is vertical.
- The top side includes several angled and stepped segments.
- A diagonal wall segment appears near the upper-left corner.
- A small open circle marks a corner near the upper-left area.

## Visible Text

The following text appears in the image:

- **PROJECTED CORNER**
- **BRACED WALL LINE 1**
- **PROJECTED LENGTH OF BRACING**
- **8' MAX. TYP.**
- **NOTE: IF THE DIAGONAL WALL IS GREATER THAN 8 FEET LONG, THEN IT MUST BE TREATED AS A SEPARATE BRACED WALL LINE**
- **BRACED WALL LINE 2**

## Detailed Annotation Description

### Top Annotations

- At the very top is a long horizontal dimension line with arrowheads at both ends labeled:
  - **BRACED WALL LINE 1**
- Beneath that is another horizontal dashed reference line labeled:
  - **PROJECTED LENGTH OF BRACING**
- Small dimension arrows appear along this projected line near the left and right portions.

### Left Annotations

- Near the upper-left corner, text reads:
  - **PROJECTED CORNER**
- A leader line points from this text to a small circular corner marker.
- Along the left side of the drawing is a vertical dimension line with arrowheads at top and bottom labeled:
  - **BRACED WALL LINE 2**

### Diagonal Segment Annotation

- A diagonal wall segment near the upper-left portion is dimensioned with a slanted dimension line and arrowheads.
- The text next to it reads:
  - **8' MAX. TYP.**

### Central Note

Centered inside the building outline is a note in uppercase text:

- **NOTE: IF THE DIAGONAL WALL IS GREATER THAN 8 FEET LONG, THEN IT MUST BE TREATED AS A SEPARATE BRACED WALL LINE**

## Shape Description

- Starting from the upper-left projected corner area, the wall drops diagonally down and right.
- It then transitions into a horizontal top segment.
- Near the middle top, the wall jogs downward and then back horizontally.
- Farther right, it jogs upward again before continuing to the top-right corner.
- The right wall extends vertically downward.
- The bottom wall runs horizontally across the full width.
- The left wall runs vertically upward to meet the diagonal upper-left segment.
